description: The intrinsic, inalienable worth possessed by all beings, independent of status, ability, or achievement. It is not something to be earned or lost, but a fundamental quality to be recognized and honored in oneself and others.
rationale: A composition of the roots 'ka' (to stand, to be present) and 'lo' (connection, relation). The word suggests 'standing in relation' or 'a worthy presence.' The firm 'k' sound gives it a sense of uprightness and strength, while the flowing 'lo' provides a graceful, relational quality, embodying both inner self-possession and outward respect.
